key_insight: The paper shows that pretrained physics-augmented neural networks can be deployed as practical hyperelastic constitutive laws inside explicit finite element codes without requiring a custom solver.
engineering_relevance: This enables engineers to use machine-learning-based material models in impact simulations while preserving physics constraints and keeping the workflow compatible with existing Radioss and OpenRadioss pipelines.
method: Train hyperelastic PANNs in PyTorch with embedded physical constraints.
Export the trained architecture and parameters into standalone Fortran user material routines.
Optimize runtime by swapping SoftPlus for the cheaper SQuarePlus activation.
evidence_signal: An impact simulation example shows the generated PANN user material reproduces the nonlinear large-strain response characteristic of hyperelastic materials, and the paper evaluates how network architecture affects simulation performance.
watch_item: Deployment still depends on careful network design and activation choice, since evaluation cost can impact explicit-solver efficiency and stability.
paper_title: Implementation of Hyperelastic Physics-Augmented Neural Networks in the Explicit Finite Element Codes Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss with Applications to Impact Events
